name: DOEON KIM
headline: AI Solutions Architect | Governed Delivery, Data Workflows, Operational AI
summary: Designs and delivers reviewable AI systems from discovery and pilot planning to governed rollout, with public proof in enterprise delivery, multimodal operations, and operator-facing workflows.
Turns ambiguous requirements into inspectable architectures with clear trust boundaries, review paths, and handoff artifacts instead of one-off demos.

Pairs hands-on implementation with customer-facing architecture judgment across cloud, data, runtime, and governance-heavy delivery contexts, including live workshop preview, warehouse proof, and verified reviewer lanes.
Certified across Microsoft, Snowflake, Databricks, and Palantir-aligned stacks, with public proof surfaces that let reviewers inspect shipped systems quickly.

bullets: Delivered project work across RAG, incident operations, and real-time operator workflows under practitioner and Microsoft Korea feedback loops.
Converted ambiguous project requirements into reviewable system designs, live demos, and handoff-ready delivery surfaces across multiple team systems.
Improved Haptix multi-label performance to AP 96.8 on single-tag and AP 85.0 on triple-tag cases while building backend and retrieval delivery surfaces.
Built customer-facing full-stack features and integrated OpenAI-powered chatbot workflows for a US startup product environment.
Worked asynchronously across US time zones and kept delivery moving across application, AI integration, and stakeholder communication boundaries.

experience.role: Strategic Backbone Network & Security Team Lead
bullets: Led a 6-person squad in a 24/7 mission-critical environment and kept operations reviewable under no-fail expectations.
Handled roughly 8-9 incidents per month, leading triage, mitigation, IAM/access control, emergency recovery, and compliance-oriented handoff.

projects.name: Enterprise LLM Adoption Kit
bullets: Implemented RBAC, prompt-injection detection, PII redaction, audit logging, JSONL eval pipelines, and rollout artifacts.
Added rollout-gates, live-workshop-preview, and customer-architecture-pack surfaces to support discovery, pilot, rollout, and handoff decisions.

projects.name: Nexus-Hive
bullets: Built governed analytics review surfaces with warehouse brief, reviewer-query-demo, semantic governance, and target scorecards for SQLite, Snowflake, and Databricks-aligned review.

projects.name: Lakehouse Contract Lab
bullets: Built explicit Spark + Delta medallion proof with quality gates, reviewer-readable table previews, and artifact-refresh review summaries for Snowflake- and Databricks-style architecture conversations.

projects.name: secure-xl2hwp-local
bullets: Built a local spreadsheet-to-document workflow with signed export verification, readiness checks, and offline-deployment-pack surfaces for constrained environments.

projects.name: AegisOps
bullets: Combined logs, screenshots, RCA, postmortem-pack, escalation-readiness, and live escalation preview surfaces into a reviewer-safe incident workflow.

value_i_bring: Translate technical ambiguity into reviewable systems with clearer operating boundaries and proof.
Work well across engineers, specialists, and customer-facing stakeholders to support trusted delivery.
Pair architecture judgment with enough implementation depth to build the proof surface, not just describe it.
